book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.arcan: The arrays catalogued in this chapter range from simple [#](8B0000)crafting arrays[#]() that reshape matter, to complex inscriptions that forge [#](4A0080)sigils[#](), bind [#](4A0080)sentient armour[#](), and even bend the passage of time. Master the fundamentals, and the rest shall follow.
The foundation of all vitaemantic craft, circles inscribed by the scribe's hand.

A second item placed becomes the [#](8B0000)catalyst[#](). If both are valid, the array awakens and its working begins. Both components are [#](8B0000)consumed[#]() as the array activates; nothing remains but the result of the working.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Watch the pattern carefully when you place the base. If the glyph changes form, you are on the right path.[#]()

Before sigils, before rituals, before the great workings of [#](4A0080)Vitaemancy[#]() were ever conceived, there was the [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#](). Inscribed with the [#](8B0000)Arcane Scribe Tool[#]() upon bare stone or earth, the array is the most fundamental expression of the art: a circle of intent, waiting to be given purpose.

To inscribe an array, hold the [#](8B0000)Arcane Scribe Tool[#]() and press [Use] upon a solid surface. Each inscription consumes one charge; the tool holds twenty uses before wearing out.\
\
The empty array is inert. Click it with an item to place the [#](8B0000)base[#]() component. The design shifts if the base is recognized.
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.craft: The simplest array, inscribe essence onto matter and reshape it.

Some crafting arrays require only common materials. Others demand reagents forged through more advanced means; the complexity of the product mirrors the complexity of the path to reach it.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Most sigils, reagents, and bindings begin their existence within a crafting array. Master this foundation well.[#]()

[#](8B0000)Crafting Arrays[#]() are among the simplest expressions of the art. The array inscribes the essence of the [#](8B0000)base[#]() onto the [#](8B0000)catalyst[#](), transmuting both into something new. A brief, elegant animation accompanies the transformation.
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.defen: Wards that repel foes and deflect projectiles.

The Deflection Array requires an [#](8B0000)Tabula Animata[#]() and a diamond to inscribe, reflecting its advanced nature. It protects a column roughly 6 blocks tall and 3 blocks wide.\
\
The Repulsion Array needs only iron and lapis, making it accessible even to apprentice vitaemancers.\
\
[#](4A0080)The best defense is one your enemy never sees.[#]()

Where the Spike Array punishes intruders with pain, these arrays offer more sophisticated defenses.\
\
The [#](8B0000)Repulsion Array[#]() creates an invisible ward that pushes hostile creatures away in a 5-block radius. Anything that means you harm will find itself unable to approach.\
\
The [#](8B0000)Deflection Array[#]() projects a column of protective force above it, reflecting any projectile that passes through back the way it came.
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.downg: The armour craves combat; starve it of violence, and it starves you.

The armour develops a bloodlust that seeps into your belly. If you fail to attack something recently, gnawing hunger overtakes you. Higher levels shorten the grace period and intensify the starvation.\
\
[#](4A0080)The armour feeds on violence. Deny it, and it feeds on you instead.[#]()
Battle Hungry
Battle Hungry
The armour drags you down; water becomes a grave.

The armour grows dense and waterlogged, dragging you down through liquid like an anchor. Reduces swim speed by up to 80%%.\
\
[#](4A0080)The depths call, and the armour answers on your behalf.[#]()
Concrete Shoes
Concrete Shoes
The armour locks your off-hand; one arm must suffice.

The armour seizes your off-hand in a rigid grip, preventing you from using whatever you hold there. No torches, no shields, no secondary tools.\
\
[#](4A0080)The armour demands your undivided attention.[#]()
Crippled Arm
Crippled Arm
The armour festers, choking your body's ability to heal.

The armour's living tissue sickens, suppressing your body's ability to mend. All healing is reduced by up to 80%%; a wound that should restore ten hearts barely returns two.\
\
[#](4A0080)What lives can also sicken.[#]()
Diseased
Diseased
The armour bleeds your strength; every strike lands softer.

The armour saps the force behind your strikes, blunting every blow. Reduces melee damage by up to 80%%.\
\
[#](4A0080)Your sword arm weakens as the armour feeds elsewhere.[#]()
Dulled Blade
Dulled Blade
The armour drags at your arms; mining becomes a crawl.

The armour weighs upon your arms, making every swing of the pick feel like moving through tar. Reduces dig speed by up to 80%%.\
\
[#](4A0080)The stone mocks your feeble blows.[#]()
Leadened Pick
Leadened Pick
The armour hobbles your stride; each step heavier than the last.

The armour stiffens around your legs, hobbling your stride. Reduces movement speed by up to 70%%.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10\
\
[#](4A0080)Every step is a negotiation with the armour's weight.[#]()
Limp Leg
Limp Leg
The armour seals your throat; no potion shall pass your lips.

The armour rejects all liquid sustenance, clamping your throat shut against any potion you attempt to consume. No healing draughts, no buffs, no alchemical aids.\
\
[#](4A0080)The armour alone shall sustain you, or so it believes.[#]()
Quenched
Quenched
The armour ruins your aim; every shot goes astray.

The armour interferes with your aim, introducing wild inaccuracy to every arrow and bolt you loose. Bows and crossbows become exercises in frustration.\
\
[#](4A0080)Your hands are steady. The armour is not.[#]()
Storm Trooper
Storm Trooper
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.envir: Arrays that command weather, growth, and frost.

The [#](8B0000)Freeze Array[#]() reaches out to nearby water sources and converts them to ice, while covering exposed solid ground in a thin layer of snow. The effect spreads in a 3-block radius.\
\
Once the array has frozen everything in reach, it dissipates.\
\
[#](4A0080)The vitaemancer's will made manifest in frost and bloom.[#]()

These arrays reshape the world around them.\
\
The [#](8B0000)Tempest Array[#]() commands the weather, toggling rain on or off at a cost of 500 EV from the owner's soul network. Like the time arrays, it is consumed on use.\
\
The [#](8B0000)Growth Array[#]() coaxes nearby crops and plants to grow faster in a 2-block radius, applying a gentle acceleration each second.

The [#](8B0000)Undertow Array[#]() is scribed with [#](8B0000)Kelp[#]() and awakened by [#](8B0000)Redstone Dust[#](). Once active, it opens a [#](8B0000)vitaemantic current[#]() through the open column of air directly above the glyph, lifting or drowning any entity that enters, no water required.\
\
[#](8B0000)Right-click with an empty hand[#]() to reverse the flow. Upward currents push entities skyward; downward currents drag them to the bottom, mirroring a magma block's pull. The array is persistent and remembers its direction across world reloads.\
\
[#](8B0000)Scaling[#](): the strength of the current is read live from the array's own slots. Adding more [#](8B0000)Redstone Dust[#]() to the redstone slot strengthens the acceleration; adding more [#](8B0000)Kelp[#]() to the kelp slot raises the terminal velocity. Simply right-click the array with additional ingredients to stack them into place.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Tip: the Arcane Scribe Tool can inscribe arrays directly into water source blocks, so an Undertow Array can still be placed at the bottom of a pool without draining it first.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.impri: Rebinds a monster spawner to a creature slain beside it.

The [#](8B0000)Array of Imprisonment[#]() binds a wandering soul into stone. Inscribe it directly atop a [#](8B0000)Monster Spawner[#](), then slay a creature within an [#](8B0000)11x11x11[#]() region centred on the array.\
\
The spawner forgets its old charge and takes on the dying creature's nature instead. The glyph, its purpose fulfilled, crumbles to nothing.

Pair it with a [#](4A0080)Torment Nexus[#]() to grow a captive harvest of whatever quarry you choose. Reconfiguring a spawner is a one-time rite: to change it again, inscribe a fresh array.\
\
A few mighty creatures are too willful to be bound and the array ignores their passing.\
\
[#](4A0080)What dies near the cage becomes the cage.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.loyal: Inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() using a [#](4A0080)Lead[#]() as the base reagent and a [#](4A0080)Tabula Robur[#]() as the catalyst. Step near the active array to trigger the summoning. The nearest player within 8 blocks will be treated as the owner.
Summon and revive your tamed companions.

The [#](8B0000)Array of Loyal Friends[#]() is a powerful restorative circle that calls all of your tamed companions to your side. Any living pet within range will be teleported to the array and ordered to follow you.\
\
More remarkably, the array reaches beyond death itself. Fallen pets are restored to full health at the array's location, though they lose any items they were carrying. The array costs [#](4A0080)5,000 EV[#]() from your soul network.
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.movem: Arrays of force, hurl creatures and objects with vitaemantic velocity.

A pair of arrays designed to hurl anything that crosses their threshold, players, creatures, loose items alike. One propels horizontally in the direction the entity faces; the other launches straight upward.

[#](8B0000)Speed Array[#](): Launches entities horizontally in the direction they face. Inscribed via [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]().\
\
[#](8B0000)Updraft Array[#](): Hurls entities skyward with considerable force. Inscribed via [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]().\
\
[#](2E8B57)Useful for rapid transit systems, mob processing, or simply entertaining oneself at a guest's expense.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.name: Alchemy Arrays
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.redst: Arrays that interface with redstone mechanisms.

These arrays bridge the gap between vitaemancy and redstone engineering.\
\
The [#](8B0000)Signal Array[#]() outputs a redstone signal from 0 to 15, proportional to the Essentia Vitae stored in the owner's soul network. It scales linearly up to 1,000,000 EV. Requires a bound [#](8B0000)Arcane Ash[#]() to function.

The [#](8B0000)Trigger Array[#]() emits a brief redstone pulse whenever a mob or player steps on it. The pulse lasts half a second, long enough for any standard redstone mechanism to detect.\
\
It functions much like a pressure plate, but is nearly invisible once inscribed.\
\
[#](4A0080)The blood knows who walks above it.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.senti:
The [#](8B0000)Ritual of Binding[#](): [#](8B0000)Binding Reagent[#]() as base, an iron armour piece as catalyst.\
\
The armour is alive, and it watches everything you do. Be deliberate in what you teach it. [#](2E8B57)Hold sneak while examining a piece to see what it has learned so far.[#]()

Inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#](), then apply the [#](8B0000)Binding Reagent[#]() as base. Place your [#](8B0000)Iron Helmet[#](), [#](8B0000)Iron Chestplate[#](), [#](8B0000)Iron Leggings[#](), or [#](8B0000)Iron Boots[#]() as catalyst, and step back.\
\
The armour begins equivalent to iron, but possesses 100 [#](B8860B)Upgrade Points[#]() that it will spend as it grows alongside you. There may be ways to surpass this limitation...
Armour that breathes, learns, and grows alongside its wearer.

To create [#](8B0000)Sentient Equipment[#](), you require [#](8B0000)Iron Armour[#](), the [#](8B0000)Arcane Scribe Tool[#](), a [#](8B0000)Binding Reagent[#](), and at least a [#](8B0000)Common Spiritus Gem[#]() charged with sufficient [#](4A0080)Spiritus[#](). What emerges is no longer mere metal; it is alive, and it learns from you.

The [#](8B0000)Binding Reagent[#]() is forged in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#]().\
\
[#](8B0000)Sentient Equipment[#]() rivals [#](B8860B)Diamond[#]() in resilience and can be repaired in an [#](8B0000)Anvil[#]() with additional [#](8B0000)Binding Reagent[#]().
The armour learns from your deeds; guide its growth with care.

You have felt it, the armour shifting, adapting, straining to assist you in whatever task it observes. It grows in many directions at once, but its capacity is finite. Trying to master everything yields mastery of nothing.

Consider forging multiple sets, each trained for a specific purpose. A miner's set, a warrior's set, an explorer's set; specialization is the path to true power.\
\
You have devised [#](8B0000)Rituals[#]() to assist with focused training, and another to imbue your armour with a greater capacity for growth.

The armour banks experience toward a skill each time you perform the deed that teaches it, and advances a level once enough has gathered.\
\
[#](B8860B)By magnitude[#](): skills tied to harm, healing, knowledge, or travel learn in proportion to the event, the damage taken or dealt, the health mended, the experience gathered, the distance crossed. Greater deeds teach it faster.

[#](B8860B)By repetition[#](): skills like mining, or enduring fire and venom, learn a fixed measure each time, one mark per block broken, or one for every moment wreathed in flame or coursing with poison.\
\
Each skill rises at its own thresholds. A [#](8B0000)few[#]() are never taught through deeds at all, but inscribed directly from a Tome.
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.sigil: Command the wind to hurl you skyward, at your own peril.

The [#](8B0000)Air Sigil[#]() hurls you through the sky in whatever direction you face, trading 50 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() for each burst of momentum. The wind obeys, but gravity does not; this sigil offers no protection from the landing.\
\
[#](2E8B57)More than one overconfident apprentice has drained their [#](8B0000)Anima[#]() mid-flight. Know your reserves before you leap.[#]()
Air Sigil
Air Sigil

Forge the [#](8B0000)Air Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a slate as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)The sky remembers every mage who forgot to look down.[#]()

The sigil remembers a [#](8B0000)brightness level[#]() from 1 to 15. Left-click the air to cycle the brightness upward. Sneak and left-click to cycle it downward. The current setting is shown in the sigil's tooltip.\
\
Once a blood light has been placed, you can also adjust it directly. Right-click an existing blood light with an empty hand to increase its brightness, or sneak and right-click to dim it.
Brightness Control
Cast colored motes of blood-light into the darkness ahead of you.

Blood lights are not limited to their natural crimson. Combine the sigil with any vanilla [#](8B0000)dye[#]() in a shapeless craft to change its color. All other data on the sigil, including its binding and brightness setting, is preserved.\
\
Lights placed after dyeing will glow in the chosen color. To restore the default red, simply place the sigil alone in a crafting grid.
Color Dyeing

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of the Blood Lamp[#]() launches a mote of crystallized [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() in the direction you face. When it strikes a surface, it anchors itself as a permanent floating light source at a cost of merely 10 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]().\
\
Alternatively, right-clicking a nearby surface places the light directly without launching a projectile.
Sigil of the Blood Lamp
Sigil of the Blood Lamp

Forge the [#](8B0000)Blood Lamp Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a slate as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)A small light to banish the deepest dark, in any color you desire.[#]()

Right-click a blood light with [#](8B0000)Redstone Dust[#]() to toggle redstone sensitivity. When enabled, the light will only emit light and particles while receiving a redstone signal. Without a signal, it goes dark.\
\
Right-click with redstone again to disable redstone control and return the light to its always-on state.
Redstone Control
Inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with an [#](4A0080)Eye of Ender[#]() as the base and an [#](4A0080)Tabula Animata[#]() as the catalyst.
Crafting
Access a linked container from anywhere in the world.

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of Bound Treasures[#]() links to a container and allows you to access its contents from anywhere. [#](8B0000)Shift right-click[#]() a chest, barrel, or any other container to bind it to the sigil. Then [#](8B0000)right-click[#]() in the air to open the linked container remotely at a cost of [#](4A0080)200 EV[#]().\
\
The linked container must be in a loaded chunk to be accessed. If the container is destroyed, the link is broken.
Sigil of Bound Treasures
Sigil of Bound Treasures

Sigils fall into two schools of invocation:

- [#](8B0000)Use-on-cast[#]() sigils such as the [#](8B0000)Water[#](), [#](8B0000)Lava[#](), [#](8B0000)Void[#](), and [#](8B0000)Blood Lamp Sigils[#]() perform a single act each time you invoke them, drawing a fixed measure of [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() per use.

- [#](8B0000)Toggleable[#]() sigils maintain a continuous effect while held active. They draw [#](4A0080)EV[#]() at a steady upkeep so long as their power flows.
Sigil Activation

The toggleable sigils are:

- [#](8B0000)Sigil of the Green Grove[#]()

- [#](8B0000)Sigil of the Fast Miner[#]()

- [#](8B0000)Sigil of Magnetism[#]()

- [#](8B0000)Sigil of the Frozen Lake[#]()

- [#](8B0000)Sigil of Suppression[#]()

- [#](8B0000)Sigil of the Phantom Bridge[#]()

To toggle a sigil on or off, hold [#](8B0000)Shift[#]() and right-click into open air. Aiming at a block or creature will perform that sigil's normal action instead, so the shift requirement protects against accidental toggles in the heat of work.

An active toggleable sigil bears a faint enchantment shimmer, and its tooltip lists the [#](4A0080)EV[#]() it drains per interval. When your [#](8B0000)Anima[#]() runs dry, the sigil quietly deactivates until you refill and reignite it.
A lens into the unseen, reveals the state of your Anima and Ara Vitae.

Hold sneak and press [Use] to open a configuration interface. Here you may reposition the various [#](4A0080)Vitaemancy[#]() display elements on your field of vision. Click and drag to move them, then select Save to preserve the arrangement, or Default to restore the original positions.

The configurable displays are:

- The Incense Altar (light grey)

- The Seer's Sigil (purple)

- The Divination Sigil (lavender)

- The Spiritus Aura Gauge (orange)

- The Sigil of Holding (green)

Press [#](4A0080)H[#]() (rebindable under Controls) to drag each display where you like.

The [#](8B0000)Divination Sigil[#]() is the first lens through which a Vitaemancer peers into the invisible currents of [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() that sustain all vitaemantic works. Forged in an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() from humble components, it reveals what the naked eye cannot.
Divination Sigil
Divination Sigil

Inscribe the array with [#](8B0000)Redstone Dust[#]() as the base and a [#](8B0000)Tabula Rasa[#]() as the catalyst. The sigil that emerges thrums faintly with awareness.\
\
[#](4A0080)To see is to begin understanding.[#]()

The sigil serves two vital purposes:

- Activate it toward open air to reveal the [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() stored within your [#](8B0000)Anima[#](), the measure of your reserves.

- Aim it at an [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() to reveal the altar's current tier, the volume of [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() within, and its maximum capacity.
Feed life into the soil and watch the world bloom around you.

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of the Green Grove[#]() channels [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() into the living earth, coaxing plants to grow with unnatural speed. A marriage of blood and botany that any practitioner with a garden will find indispensable.
Sigil of the Green Grove
Sigil of the Green Grove
Forge the [#](8B0000)Growth Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a [#](8B0000)Tabula Robur[#]() as catalyst.

Aim at a growable block and press [Use] to apply a burst of growth for 150 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]().\
\
Alternatively, hold sneak and press [Use] to toggle a persistent aura. While active, every block within a 7x7x5 volume centered on you receives a growth pulse every 5 seconds, at a cost of 150 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() per cycle.
Carry five sigils in one, their power at your fingertips.

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of Holding[#]() is a vessel within a vessel, capable of containing up to five other [#](8B0000)Sigils[#]() simultaneously. While held, it channels their passive effects and allows you to activate any of them at will.\
\
Press the Open Holding keybind to access its inventory. Use the cycle keybinds or hold sneak and scroll to switch between stored sigils.
Sigil of Holding
Sigil of Holding

Forge the [#](8B0000)Holding Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a slate as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)One hand to hold the world's power.[#]()
Walk upon water made solid by the chill of your Anima.

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of the Frozen Lake[#]() chills the water beneath your feet as you walk, crystallizing it into solid ice within a two-block radius. The effect is reminiscent of ancient frost enchantments, but drawn from [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() rather than arcane inscriptions.\
\
Toggle it with sneak and [Use]. The ice endures only as long as you remain near.
Sigil of the Frozen Lake
Sigil of the Frozen Lake
Summon molten stone at a steep cost to your Anima.

Sister to the [#](8B0000)Water Sigil[#](), the [#](8B0000)Lava Sigil[#]() draws molten stone from the depths of your [#](8B0000)Anima[#]() at a cost of 1,000 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#](). The price reflects the violence of the element, and if your reserves cannot bear it, five hearts are torn from your flesh instead.
Lava Sigil
Lava Sigil

Forge the [#](8B0000)Lava Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a [#](8B0000)Tabula Rasa[#]() as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)Handle with the reverence due to hellfire.[#]()

Like its cooler counterpart, the [#](8B0000)Lava Sigil[#]() may serve as a component in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#]() to automate the production of [#](8B0000)Lava Buckets[#](). The sigil is not consumed.
Extend your reach; items drift toward you unbidden.

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of Magnetism[#]() bends the subtle forces between you and nearby objects, extending your item pickup range to 7 blocks while active. Hold the sigil, then hold sneak and press [Use] to toggle the effect.\
\
The sigil drains 50 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() every 5 seconds while its pull persists.
Sigil of Magnetism
Sigil of Magnetism

Forge the [#](8B0000)Magnetism Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a slate as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)The world draws near to those who command it.[#]()
Blood-fueled haste for those who delve deep.

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of the Fast Miner[#]() infuses your muscles with vitaemantic vigor, granting the Haste effect while active. Stone yields faster, timber falls quicker, earth parts more readily.\
\
Toggle with sneak and [Use]. Drains 100 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() every 5 seconds while the effect persists.
Sigil of the Fast Miner
Sigil of the Fast Miner

Forge the [#](8B0000)Mining Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a [#](8B0000)Tabula Robur[#]() as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)The earth parts before the blood-quickened hand.[#]()

Summoned undead are loyal to their creator. They will follow you when idle and automatically attack whatever target you last struck. They cannot harm their master, drop no loot or experience, and are immune to sunlight.\
\
To dismiss a servant early, [#](8B0000)shift right-click[#]() it while holding the sigil.
Servant Behavior

Unlike most sigils, the [#](8B0000)Sigil of Necromancy[#]() is forged in the [#](8B0000)Athanor[#]() rather than inscribed via alchemy array. Place a [#](4A0080)Tabula Robur[#](), [#](4A0080)Rotten Flesh[#](), and [#](4A0080)Bone[#]() in the input slots with a [#](8B0000)Resonator[#]() tool. Supply [#](4A0080)1,000 mB of Essentia Vitae[#]() and a minimum of [#](8B0000)20 Raw Spiritus[#]().
Crafting
Raise undead servants to fight alongside you.

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of Necromancy[#]() calls upon the latent death in the world to raise an [#](4A0080)Undead Servant[#]() at the location you are aiming. Each use costs [#](4A0080)2,000 EV[#]() and summons one of four undead variants at random: a zombie, husk, skeleton, or stray.\
\
Servants last for 5 minutes before crumbling to dust, and will disappear if they stray too far from their master.
Sigil of Necromancy
Sigil of Necromancy
Walk upon crystallized EV, a bridge born of blood beneath your feet.

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of the Phantom Bridge[#]() crystallizes [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() into temporary platforms beneath your feet as you walk. These spectral surfaces allow you to traverse chasms, voids, and open sky with the confidence of solid ground.\
\
The phantom blocks dissolve shortly after you leave them behind. [#](2E8B57)Keep moving; hesitation over the abyss is unwise.[#]()
Sigil of the Phantom Bridge
Sigil of the Phantom Bridge

Forge the [#](8B0000)Phantom Bridge Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a slate as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)Between you and the void, blood is the only bridge you need.[#]()
A deeper sight, reveals the full workings of the Ara Vitae.

When aimed at an [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#](), the Seer's display reveals, from top to bottom:

- The altar's current [#](B8860B)Tier[#]()

- Current [#](4A0080)EV[#]() volume and total capacity

- Active crafting progress, if any

- [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() consumed per tick during crafting

- Current [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() reserve held by Charging Runes

Where the [#](8B0000)Divination Sigil[#]() offers a glimpse, the [#](8B0000)Seer's Sigil[#]() opens your perception wide. It reveals not only the [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() within your [#](8B0000)Anima[#](), but lays bare the inner workings of the [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() itself , crafting progress, consumption rates, and more.\
\
It also provides the same display configuration as its lesser counterpart.
Seer's Sigil
Seer's Sigil

First, forge the [#](8B0000)Sight Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](). Then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a slate as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)When mere sight is no longer sufficient.[#]()

The Seer also lays bare the reach of your [#](4A0080)rituals[#](). Look upon an active [#](8B0000)Master Ritual Stone[#]() and the ritual's working area is traced in the world, a coloured boundary drawn around each of its ranges, exactly the zones the [#](8B0000)Ritual Configurator[#]() defines.\
\
[#](2E8B57)The sigil need only be on your person: in your pack, tucked within a Sigil of Holding, or worn as a charm.[#]()
Command the elements to part; fluid yields to your presence.

The [#](8B0000)Sigil of Suppression[#]() exerts your will upon the elements themselves, temporarily banishing all fluids within roughly six blocks of you. The liquid vanishes as though it never existed, returning only after you move away.\
\
[#](2E8B57)A powerful tool for traversing Nether lava oceans. But be wary of deep water; deactivating above a chasm invites a swift end.[#]()
Sigil of Suppression
Sigil of Suppression

Forge the [#](8B0000)Suppression Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a slate as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)The elements themselves bow before sufficient will.[#]()
Tear through space itself to reach a bound Teleposer.

The [#](8B0000)Teleposition Sigil[#]() tears a momentary rift between you and a linked [#](8B0000)Teleposer[#](), pulling you across any distance, even between dimensions, for 1,000 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]().\
\
Sneak-click on a [#](8B0000)Teleposer[#]() to bind its location to the sigil. [#](2E8B57)Do not move the Teleposer afterward, or you may find yourself arriving somewhere... unexpected.[#]()
Teleposition Sigil
Teleposition Sigil

Forge the [#](8B0000)Teleposition Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a slate as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)The distance between here and there is merely a matter of blood.[#]()
Erase fluid from existence with a single gesture.

The [#](8B0000)Void Sigil[#]() annihilates fluid on contact, consuming 50 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() per block erased. Aim at any liquid and press [Use] to watch it vanish without a trace.\
\
Invaluable for clearing treacherous lava flows or unwanted floods without the tedium of conventional methods.
Void Sigil
Void Sigil

Forge the [#](8B0000)Void Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a slate as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)What the void takes, it does not return.[#]()
Conjure water from the currents of your Anima.

The [#](8B0000)Water Sigil[#]() conjures a source of water from nothing, drawn from the currents of your [#](8B0000)Anima[#]() at a cost of 100 [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() per use. Aim at any solid surface and press [Use].\
\
Should your reserves prove insufficient, the sigil draws the toll from your own blood instead.
Water Sigil
Water Sigil

Forge the [#](8B0000)Water Reagent[#]() in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), then inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() with the reagent as base and a [#](8B0000)Tabula Rasa[#]() as catalyst.\
\
[#](4A0080)An endless spring, born of sacrifice.[#]()

The [#](8B0000)Water Sigil[#]() may also serve as a component in the [#](8B0000)Tabula Vitae[#](), automating the production of [#](8B0000)Water Buckets[#](). The sigil itself is not consumed in this process.
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.spike: An invisible ward that punishes those who tread upon it.

The [#](8B0000)Spike Array[#]() is a cruel but effective ward. Any living creature that steps onto it suffers a full heart of damage. The array is nearly invisible once inscribed, making it ideal for defending passages or feeding certain... automated arrangements.

Inscribe an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]() and apply the appropriate base and catalyst to form the spike glyph.\
\
[#](4A0080)The floor remembers every trespass.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.spiri: A trap that damages mobs and harvests raw spiritus.

Requires an [#](8B0000)Tabula Animata[#]() to inscribe, reflecting the deeper understanding of spiritual energy needed to harness it.\
\
Each activation releases 0.5 units of raw (default) spiritus into the chunk. Players are immune to its effects.\
\
[#](4A0080)Every creature carries a spark of will. This array simply... liberates it.[#]()

This array combines the cruelty of the Spike Array with a deeper purpose. When a non-player creature steps onto it, the array inflicts a full heart of damage and extracts a fragment of raw [#](8B0000)Spiritus[#]() from the act of suffering, releasing it into the surrounding chunk.\
\
The array has a brief cooldown between activations to prevent overwhelming the local aura with too much spiritus at once.
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.telep: A vertical transit glyph that folds space between aligned arrays.

Multiple arrays can be chained vertically to create a multi-floor elevator shaft. Each array searches only along the same X/Z column, so they must be precisely aligned.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Place one at the bottom of your base and one at the top for instant vertical transit. Add intermediate floors as needed, each jump or sneak moves you to the nearest array, not the furthest.[#]()

The [#](8B0000)Teleposition Array[#]() folds space between two aligned glyphs, creating a seamless vertical transit system. A single array does nothing on its own you must place [#](8B0000)at least two[#]() arrays directly above one another, aligned on the same block column, at any distance up to [#](B8860B)64 blocks[#]().\
\
[#](B8860B)Jump[#]() while standing on an array to teleport upward to the next array above. [#](B8860B)Sneak[#]() to descend to the nearest array below.
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.time_: Bend the sky's clock, summon dawn or hasten dusk.

Among the most audacious workings in [#](4A0080)Vitaemancy[#]() are these arrays that bend the passage of time itself. Once activated, the inscribed components are consumed as the sky obeys.\
\
The [#](8B0000)Day[#]() array advances the world to the next sunrise. The [#](8B0000)Night[#]() array draws it forward to the next sunset.

[#](8B0000)New Dawn[#](): Inscribed via [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#](). Commands the sun to rise.\
\
[#](8B0000)True Twilight[#](): Inscribed via [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#](). Beckons the fall of night.\
\
[#](4A0080)Time is not yours to own, only to borrow.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.train: A focus of discipline, control what your sentient armour learns.

The armour's undirected growth can be maddening. To impose order upon it, you have devised the [#](8B0000)Training Bracelet[#](), a focus that allows you to dictate which abilities the armour should pursue and which it should ignore.\
\
Press [Use] to open its configuration menu.

The [#](8B0000)Training Bracelet[#]() is forged in an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#]().\
\
Only one bracelet is active at a time. Priority order: off-hand, Curios slots (if available), main inventory, then any add-on inventories.

With a copy of the relevant [#](8B0000)Tome[#]() in hand, you can set a level cap for any upgrade. Once the armour reaches that limit, it ceases training that skill.\
\
[#](2E8B57)To allow all upgrades except one: set the bracelet to 'allow others' mode and cap the unwanted skill at level 0.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.upgra: Feed yourself well and the armour fortifies your body in turn.

The armour observes your habits of nourishment and responds in kind, hardening your frame against impact. Grants an [#](4A0080)anchored stance[#]() that resists being thrown and bolsters your vitality considerably.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Eating food.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 5
Body Builder
Body Builder
Inscribe knowledge upon tomes, and the armour crystallizes into diamond-like hardness.

The armour responds to the knowledge you inscribe upon [#](8B0000)Upgrade Tomes[#](), hardening its lattice with each lesson. Grants up to +5 [#](4A0080)Armour[#]() and +10 [#](4A0080)Toughness[#]().\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Inscribe a [#](8B0000)Brilliance Tome[#]() in the Tabula Vitae, then apply it to Living Armour. Each tome grants 1 level, up to a maximum of 6.
Brilliance
Brilliance
Your momentum becomes a weapon; the faster you charge, the harder you strike.

The armour learns to channel your momentum into devastating force. Sprinting attacks deal increased damage and knockback, up to +50%%.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Dealing damage while sprinting.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 5
Charging Strike
Charging Strike
The armour grows sockets to hold your vitaemantic trinkets.

The armour opens hidden recesses within itself, forming [#](8B0000)Sentient Armour Socket[#]() slots that can hold compatible [#](4A0080)Vitaemancy[#]() trinkets.\
\
This upgrade cannot be trained; only inscribed via [#](8B0000)Upgrade Tome[#](). Each tome adds one additional socket.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Requires the Curios API.[#]()
Socketed
Socketed
The deeper you dig, the faster the armour drives your arms.

The armour recognizes the rhythm of the pick. Mining identical blocks in succession accelerates your speed, and at higher levels a surge of [#](4A0080)Haste[#]() follows each broken block.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Mining blocks.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10
Dwarven Might
Dwarven Might
Wings of living tissue unfurl from the chestplate, take to the sky.

The armour sprouts membranes of living tissue, granting true flight. This upgrade cannot be trained; it must be inscribed via [#](8B0000)Upgrade Tome[#]().\
\
The wings draw upon the chestplate's vitality, wearing it down at half the rate of conventional elytra. The aesthetic is, admittedly, rather striking.
Elytra
Elytra

The [#](8B0000)Elytra Tome[#]() is forged in an [#](8B0000)Alchemy Array[#](). Apply it to your [#](8B0000)Sentient Chestplate[#]() to unfurl the wings.\
\
[#](4A0080)Blood gave you life. Now it gives you the sky.[#]()
The armour draws deeper knowledge from each fallen foe.

The armour learns to amplify the resonance of slain creatures, drawing forth greater experience from each kill. Increases XP drops up to 150%%.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Collecting experience orbs.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10
Experienced
Experienced
Each blow teaches the armour to sharpen the next.

The armour studies each blow you deliver and sharpens your killing edge in response. Melee attacks deal progressively greater damage.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Performing melee attacks.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10
Fierce Strike
Fierce Strike
The armour remembers the flame and learns to refuse it.

The armour absorbs the memory of flame and learns to shield you from it. Grants [#](4A0080)Fire Resistance[#](). Higher levels extend the duration and reduce the cooldown between activations.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Being on fire. [#](2E8B57)A Potion of Fire Resistance is highly recommended during training.[#]()\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 5
Gift of Ignis
Gift of Ignis
A golden sheen earns the Piglins' grudging respect.

The armour takes on a subtle golden sheen, marking you as an ally to the Piglins of the Nether. They regard you as they would any creature wearing gold.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Handing a [#](8B0000)Gold Ingot[#]() directly to a Piglin. It must be given by hand, not dropped.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 1
Gilded
Gilded
Every wound healed teaches the armour to strengthen your life force.

The armour learns from each wound mended and expands your vitality in turn, greatly increasing the amount of punishment your body can endure.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Restoring health through any means, natural regeneration, potions, or vitaemantic healing.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10
Healthy
Healthy
Every arrow absorbed teaches the armour to blunt the next.

The armour hardens against projectile impacts, blunting the force of arrows and other ranged attacks. Each level absorbs more of the blow, reducing incoming projectile damage by up to [#](4A0080)80%%[#]() at maximum.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Being struck by projectiles.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10
Pin Cushion
Pin Cushion
The armour learns to purge venom from your blood.

The armour develops an antivenom response, automatically purging Poison from your blood. Higher levels shorten the cooldown between cures.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Being poisoned.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 5
Poison Resistance
Poison Resistance
The armour quickens your step with every mile traveled.

The armour attunes itself to the cadence of your stride, propelling you ever faster. Increases movement speed up to 150%%.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Running.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10
Quick Feet
Quick Feet
The armour knits its own wounds shut, slowly restoring worn equipment.

The armour develops a regenerative instinct, periodically mending a random piece of worn equipment every 100 ticks.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Repairing the chestplate through any means, anvil, [#](8B0000)Mending[#]() enchantment, or otherwise.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 1
Repair
Repair
The armour learns to absorb impact; the ground softens beneath you.

The armour absorbs the shock of descent, cushioning your landing with increasing skill. At its peak, fall damage is negated entirely.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Suffering fall damage.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 5
Soft Fall
Soft Fall
The armour coils like a spring beneath you, launching you skyward.

The armour amplifies the power of your legs, propelling you higher with each leap. Grants up to 7.5 additional blocks of jump height and 83%% fall resistance.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Hold sneak while jumping to suppress the boost.[#]()\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Jumping.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10
Strong Legs
Strong Legs

The [#](4A0080)Tome[#]() is consumed in the process, transferring all stored experience to the chestplate. If insufficient [#](B8860B)Upgrade Points[#]() remain, the tome applies what it can and retains the rest, unless less than one level's worth remains, in which case the tome crumbles to nothing.
Crystallized knowledge, inscribe specific skills onto your sentient armour.

[#](4A0080)Upgrade Tomes[#]() are crystallized records of a particular skill, each carrying the experience needed to inscribe one or more levels of its upgrade onto a chestplate. They come from several places:\
\
- The loot tables of [#](8B0000)The Mines[#]() (ore chambers, the Smithy, Foreman's hoard).

- The [#](8B0000)Sentient Extraction[#]() ritual, which extracts the upgrades from a thrown sentient armor piece as tomes.

- Combining two tomes of the same upgrade at a vanilla crafting table.

- Inscribing select tomes, such as [#](8B0000)Brilliance[#]() and [#](8B0000)Curios Sockets[#](), in the Tabula Vitae.\
\
Press [Use] while holding a tome to inscribe one level of that skill onto your worn chestplate, assuming sufficient [#](B8860B)Upgrade Points[#]() remain. Hold sneak and press [Use] to inscribe as much as possible at once.
Upgrade Tomes
Upgrade Tomes

Tomes are the key to deliberate specialization. Focus your training on the abilities that serve you best, and discard the rest.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Consider maintaining separate chestplates for different purposes, one for mining, another for combat, a third for exploration. Each can be trained with its own set of tomes.[#]()
Pain is the teacher; each blow hardens the armour's resolve.

The armour hardens against melee strikes, explosions, and all forms of close-range violence. Protection against non-projectile harm increases with each wound endured.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Taking damage from sources other than projectiles.\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10
Tough
Tough
Calloused hands yield more Essentia Vitae with each sacrifice.

The armour toughens your hands against the bite of the sacrificial blade, yielding more [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() from each offering. Grants up to 150%% bonus to self-sacrifice.\
\
[#](B8860B)Trained by[#](): Bleeding into your [#](8B0000)Blood Orb[#]().\
\
[#](B8860B)Maximum level[#](): 10
Tough Palms
Tough Palms
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.utili: Practical arrays for collection, light, and smelting.

The [#](8B0000)Endless Fountain Array[#]() is scribed with a [#](8B0000)Block of Lapis[#]() and awakened by a [#](8B0000)Sea Pickle[#](). Once lit, it draws water from nowhere and pipes it into every fluid container touching its six faces.\
\
Each placement cycle (every 5 ticks) the array attempts to deposit [#](8B0000)up to 6 buckets[#]() of water, spread evenly across its neighbours (one per face when all six are tanks). Only whole-bucket fills are committed: if a tank has room for less than a full bucket the array skips it and tries the next cached neighbour until all 6 buckets land or every tank refuses.

The array keeps a cache of its six adjacent tanks. The cache refreshes on a timer and also reacts instantly whenever a neighbouring block is placed or broken, so tanks can be swapped in or out freely without waiting for the next scan. Neighbours in unloaded chunks are left alone until their chunk ticks in again.\
\
When every cached tank is full the array backs off progressively (like the [#](8B0000)Serenade of the Nether[#]()). While backing off it emits a small puff of drowsy slate-grey particles so you can spot a stalled fountain at a glance; the moment a tank opens up it snaps back to full cadence.\
\
A [#](8B0000)redstone signal[#]() on any face parks the array completely, preserving its cache and backoff state, making it easy to gate with a lever or comparator.

The [#](8B0000)Furnace Array[#]() transmutes raw materials dropped nearby into their smelted forms, following standard furnace recipes. It costs 10 EV per stack smelted from the owner's soul network, and items within its radius will not despawn while awaiting processing.\
\
Cook time matches a standard furnace, but the array processes all valid stacks simultaneously.\
\
[#](4A0080)The floor itself becomes the forge.[#]()

Not all arrays are weapons. These workings serve the practical needs of a vitaemancer's workshop.\
\
The [#](8B0000)Collection Array[#]() draws dropped items within 2 blocks toward its center. Place it atop a chest and collected items will be deposited directly inside.\
\
The [#](8B0000)Light Array[#]() radiates illumination from invisible sources above the array, keeping an area well-lit without cluttering it with torches.
book.neovitae.guide.alchemy_arrays.vorte: A drawing array that yanks all nearby living things toward its centre.

Several beings are beyond the sigil's grasp:\
\
- [#](2E8B57)Players in Creative or Spectator mode[#]() pass through unaffected, as expected.\
- [#](2E8B57)Any practitioner holding an Orb of Vitae[#]() - of any tier, in either main hand or off-hand - is recognised by the array and ignored. The orb's aura disrupts the drawing current. Useful for working near your own vortex without being yanked into it.\
- A [#](2E8B57)redstone signal[#]() applied to the sigil silences it entirely; nothing within the radius is pulled while the signal is active.

A drawing array, the [#](8B0000)Vortex Sigil[#]() pulls all living things toward the block directly beneath its inscription. The grip is fierce, comparable to a rocket-boosted elytra; few creatures can resist it once within its eight-block reach.\
\
Inscribed from a [#](8B0000)Tabula Robur[#]() and a [#](8B0000)Blood Pearl[#]() upon a layer of Arcane Ash.

The vortex feeds on the practitioner who bound it. Each tick of operation has a roughly [#](8B0000)1-in-100[#]() chance to draw a single [#](8B0000)EV[#]() from your Anima network. Across a full second of activity this averages out to about one fifth of an EV; a negligible cost over short sessions, but the toll accumulates if the sigil is left running for hours on end.\
\
If the bound network has nothing left to give, the vortex skips that tick - no pull, no particles - and tries again the next. The sigil remains inert in the world, waiting patiently for the well to refill.
When you first grip a bindable item and will it to life, it imprints upon your [#](4A0080)Anima[#](). From that moment forward, any [#](8B0000)Essentia Vitae[#]() cost the item demands is drawn from your network. Should the [#](4A0080)Anima[#]() run dry, some items will take payment directly from your flesh instead.\
\
Other workings, such as a hungry [#](8B0000)Ritual[#]() that has drained you completely, inflict unceasing nausea until the ritual is silenced or your [#](4A0080)Anima[#]() is replenished.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.anima.blood_sh:
Any [#](8B0000)Orb of Vitae[#]() held in the off-hand may be raised as a ward. Hold the [#](8B0000)Blood Shield[#]() key (right-click by default, rebindable in Controls) to conjure a translucent barrier of crystallised [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() before you. The ward persists for as long as you hold the key and vanishes the instant you release it.\
\
While active, the ward blocks all damage originating from your front arc, draining [#](8B0000)50 EV per second[#]() from your [#](4A0080)Anima[#]() to maintain it. If your [#](4A0080)Anima[#]() falls below [#](8B0000)200 EV[#](), the ward cannot be raised.\
\
[#](2E8B57)The ward moves with you, always positioned directly ahead. Attacks from behind or the sides will bypass it entirely.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.anima.descript: The invisible reservoir of EV that binds your soul to all vitaemantic works.
To fill your [#](4A0080)Anima[#](), you require an [#](8B0000)Orb of Vitae[#](), a crystallized anchor between your soul and the altar's power.

- Right-click with the orb in hand to sacrifice one heart, channeling [#](8B0000)200 EV[#]() into your [#](4A0080)Anima[#]().

- Place the orb within an [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() brimming with [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#](). The orb drinks deeply, limited only by your altar's [#](8B0000)Speed Runes[#]().
book.neovitae.guide.altar.anima.intro.te: Beneath the surface of the world lies an invisible lattice of living energy, your [#](4A0080)Anima[#](). This is the network that binds your soul to every sigil you wield, every ritual you ignite, every vitaemantic instrument you have ever touched. Think of it as a vast, hidden reservoir of [#](8B0000)Essentia Vitae[#](), life-force refined and stored beyond the physical realm, unique to you alone.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.anima.name: The Anima
book.neovitae.guide.altar.anima.orb_flui:
Each [#](8B0000)Orb of Vitae[#]() harbours a hidden internal reservoir of [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#](). When the altar fills your [#](4A0080)Anima[#](), a portion of that EV seeps into the orb's reservoir as well.\
\
When you place an orb containing stored fluid back upon an [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#](), the altar reverses the flow. It drains the orb's reservoir at [#](B8860B)10 times[#]() the normal fill rate, rapidly refilling the basin. If the altar is nearly full, overflow is channeled into your [#](4A0080)Anima[#]() instead. Once the orb is empty, normal behavior resumes and the altar fills your network as usual.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.anima.orb_harv:
Slay any creature while an [#](8B0000)Orb of Vitae[#]() rests in your off-hand, and the orb drinks deeply of the fallen. The victim's life force is converted into [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() and drawn directly into the orb's internal reservoir - [#](8B0000)10 EV per point of maximum health[#]().\
\
A slain zombie (20 HP) yields [#](8B0000)200 EV[#](). An Ender Dragon (200 HP) yields [#](8B0000)2,000 EV[#](). The [#](B8860B)Bonus Sacrifice[#]() attribute further multiplies this harvest.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Combine this with the orb's altar-drain behaviour: fill the orb through combat, then place it upon the Ara Vitae to rapidly replenish the basin at 10x speed.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.blan: To ascend to greater tiers, you must place [#](8B0000)Blank Runes[#]() and arrange them around the Ara Vitae in precise patterns. Specialized runes confer different blessings upon the altar's workings. The most basic, the [#](8B0000)Blank Rune[#](), carries no enchantment of its own, serving only as structural scaffolding for the Ara Vitae's ascension.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.craf:
The moment an item is laid upon the Ara Vitae, the [#](4A0080)transmutation[#]() begins. [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() drains steadily from the basin; crimson motes rising from the surface confirm the process is underway.\
\
Should the Ara Vitae run dry, gray smoke rises as the working unravels and progress is lost. The basin transmutes a [#](8B0000)single item[#]() at a time; once sufficient EV has been consumed, that item is transfigured into something new.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.desc: The sacred Ara Vitae where blood becomes power, growing through six tiers of mastery.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.feed:
An empty [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() hungers. Lay a [#](8B0000)diamond[#]() upon a bare altar and, having no stored [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() of its own, it reaches out and draws blood from any nearby who carry no [#](8B0000)Blood Orb[#](), spilling their life into the basin until the working is done. None are slain by this; the altar takes only what it needs.\
\
The basin holds [#](8B0000)10,000 EV[#]() at first. Watch the crimson pool within; its level reveals how full the vessel is. A [#](8B0000)Divination Sigil[#]() reveals the exact figures.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.firs: Your first creation should be the [#](8B0000)Novicius Orb of Vitae[#](), a diamond offered to a Tier 0 altar along with [#](8B0000)2,000 EV[#](). This orb is the key to your [#](4A0080)Anima[#](), the invisible network that binds your soul to all your vitaemantic instruments. Consult JEI for all recipes the Ara Vitae can perform.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.intr: Every practitioner of [#](4A0080)Vitaemancy[#]() must begin here, at the [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() , the Altar of Life. This sacred basin transmutes raw blood into [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#](), the refined lifeforce that fuels all vitaemantic works. It begins as a humble stone vessel, but do not be deceived: as your mastery deepens, the Ara Vitae grows into a towering monument of rune-carved power.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.name: The Ara Vitae
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.orb_: Once you hold a [#](8B0000)Blood Orb[#](), right-click it to open your veins. Standing near an [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#](), the blood flows straight into the basin; away from one, that life is stored within the orb itself to carry and pour into an altar later.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.rese: [#](2E8B57)Be aware:[#]() 10%% of the Ara Vitae's total capacity seeps into a hidden internal reservoir, used for fluid transfer operations. If your numbers seem off, this unseen vessel is likely the cause.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.tier: The simplest form. A lone Ara Vitae, unadorned by runes.
Eight runes encircle the basin. The Ara Vitae stirs with new hunger.

[#](B8860B)Tier 1[#]() demands 8 [#](8B0000)Blank Runes[#]() arranged in a ring around the Ara Vitae. The four cardinal runes may be replaced with specialized variants; the corner runes remain inert until Tier 2 unlocks their potential.
28 total runes. The altar's appetite deepens.

[#](B8860B)Tier 2[#]() expands the structure outward: 5 runes along each edge, set one level down and two blocks out from the previous ring. Stone pillars rise at each corner, capped with [#](8B0000)Blood Stained Glass[#](), light to guide the essence. Use a [#](8B0000)Divination Sigil[#]() to confirm the upgrade. Any solid block suffices for the pillar bodies.
56 total runes. The structure groans with barely-contained power.

[#](B8860B)Tier 3[#]() demands 7 runes per edge, another level down and two blocks further out. Four-block pillars rise at each corner, crowned with [#](8B0000)Bloodstone Bricks[#](), for which you will need [#](8B0000)Tau Fruit[#](), harvested from beyond the [#](4A0080)Breaching the Edge of Demon Realm[#]() ritual.
108 total runes. Few Vitaemancers dare reach this height.

[#](B8860B)Tier 4[#]() calls for 13 runes per edge, three blocks out from the previous ring, with a one-block gap at each end. [#](8B0000)Hellforged Blocks[#]() anchor the corners, rare metal wrested from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#]() itself. The Ara Vitae now commands fearsome energies.
184 total runes. The Ara Vitae becomes a cathedral of blood and stone.

[#](B8860B)Tier 5[#](), the apex of the art. 19 runes per edge sprawl outward, three blocks beyond the last ring. No corner blocks at rune level; instead, pillars ascend one tier higher, crowned with [#](8B0000)Crystal Clusters[#]() that sing with resonant energy. A monument to mastery.\
\
Forge Crystal Clusters in the [#](8B0000)Hellfire Forge[#]() from [#](8B0000)Sculk[#](), an [#](8B0000)Tabula Aetherea[#](), a [#](8B0000)Weak Blood Shard[#](), and a [#](8B0000)Nether Star[#](). [#](8B0000)Crystal Cluster Bricks[#]() also serve as valid capstones.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.ara_vitae.usag: Once placed, the [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() awakens. It drinks the blood offered to it and distills it into [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#](), which it then uses to [#](4A0080)transfigure[#]() items placed within its basin. Right-click the Ara Vitae to lay an item upon it; right-click with an empty hand to retrieve it.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.name: Ara Vitaes
book.neovitae.guide.altar.redstone_autom:
A comparator placed beside the [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() reads the level of [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() within, much like a chest.\
\
Place a [#](8B0000)Blood Stained Glass[#]() block directly beneath the altar, and the comparator instead reads the [#](4A0080)Anima[#]() of whoever owns the orb resting in the altar's basin. The signal strength scales to the orb's tier, not the maximum capacity of the network.

For example, [#](8B0000)500,000 EV[#]() would register as overflowing through a Weak Novicius Orb, but only half-strength through a Magus Orb. Clever use of this lets you shut down costly rituals before your [#](4A0080)Anima[#]() runs dry.\
\
[#](2E8B57)When the orb is bound to a team via NeoVitae Teams, the comparator reads the team's pooled Anima automatically; no special setup is needed.[#]()\
\
[#](2E8B57)Place a Redstone Lamp beneath the altar and it will emit a redstone pulse the instant a transmutation completes, useful for chained automation.[#]()
Mechanical servants for the Ara Vitae: hoppers, pipes, and comparators.

The altar also permits the transfer of [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() to and from external tanks. Connect your fluid conduit of choice and you can stockpile excess essence for later workings.\
\
[#](2E8B57)The flow is sluggish by default. Acceleration Runes quicken the altar's pulse, while Displacement Runes widen the channel through which essence flows.[#]()

Remember: the [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() piped in or out does not come directly from the altar's main basin. A hidden secondary reservoir, holding [#](8B0000)up to 10%%[#]() of the altar's total capacity, acts as the intermediary. If essence seems to vanish from the basin without explanation, or the numbers never quite add up, this phantom vessel is the culprit.

The [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() is a marvel, but even a devoted Vitaemancer tires of standing vigil over every slate. Fortunately, the altar accepts mechanical servants; items and [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() can be piped in and out.\
\
The basin holds a [#](8B0000)single item[#]() at a time. A simple [#](8B0000)Hopper[#]() feeds them in one by one, and the altar

transmutes each in turn, drawing [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() as it works. Keep the basin supplied with essence and it labors without pause.\
\
The altar makes no distinction between input and output. Without a filter, items will cycle in and out as fast as your transfer system allows. The [#](8B0000)Routing Nodes[#]() may serve you well here.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_acceler: A rune tempered in hellfire, collapsing time twice as fast.

Tempered with [#](8B0000)Netherite Scrap[#]() and [#](8B0000)Intricate Hellforged Parts[#]() from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#](), this rune warps time twice as aggressively, removing [#](8B0000)two ticks[#]() from the pulse delay instead of one. Fewer rune slots consumed, more room for other enchantments around the altar.
[#](2E8B57)Should you have need of the base rune again, the Athanor can strip the reinforcement and return it to its original form.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_aug_cap: A rune tempered in hellfire, multiplicatively stretching the altar's reservoir.

Wrought with [#](8B0000)Netherite Scrap[#]() and [#](8B0000)Intricate Hellforged Parts[#]() from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#](), this rune warps the altar's capacity to staggering proportions. Each rune multiplies the total reservoir by [#](8B0000)+15%%[#](), applied after all additive expansions. At higher tiers, the difference is immense.
[#](2E8B57)Should you have need of the base rune again, the Athanor can strip the reinforcement and return it to its original form.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_capacit: A rune tempered in hellfire, vastly deepening the altar's reservoir.

Infused with [#](8B0000)Netherite Scrap[#]() and [#](8B0000)Intricate Hellforged Parts[#]() from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#](), this rune carves a far deeper basin into the altar's soul. Each rune adds [#](8B0000)+40%%[#]() to the reservoir, additively, twice the depth of its lesser kin.
[#](2E8B57)Should you have need of the base rune again, the Athanor can strip the reinforcement and return it to its original form.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_chargin: A rune tempered in hellfire, doubling both charge rate and storage.

Hardened with [#](8B0000)Netherite Scrap[#]() and [#](8B0000)Intricate Hellforged Parts[#]() from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#](), this rune doubles the Charging Rune's potency in every regard , both the rate at which [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() is stored during idle moments and the maximum charge the rune can hold.
[#](2E8B57)Should you have need of the base rune again, the Athanor can strip the reinforcement and return it to its original form.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_disloca: A rune tempered in hellfire, vastly widening the essence flow channel.

Bolstered with [#](8B0000)Netherite Scrap[#]() and [#](8B0000)Intricate Hellforged Parts[#]() from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#](), this rune tears the channels between altar and vessel wide open. Each rune increases the flow rate by a multiplicative [#](8B0000)+40%%[#](); essence surges rather than trickles.
[#](2E8B57)Should you have need of the base rune again, the Athanor can strip the reinforcement and return it to its original form.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_orb.des: A rune tempered in hellfire, doubling the orb's capacity expansion.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_orb.int: Augmented with [#](8B0000)Netherite Scrap[#]() and [#](8B0000)Intricate Hellforged Parts[#]() from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#](), this rune resonates more deeply with the Orb of Vitae within. Each rune expands the orb's capacity by [#](8B0000)+4%%[#]() additively, twice the reach of the lesser inscription, stretching your [#](4A0080)Anima[#]() further.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_orb.rev: [#](2E8B57)Should you have need of the base rune again, the Athanor can strip the reinforcement and return it to its original form.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_sacrifi: A rune tempered in hellfire, doubling the harvest from slain creatures.

Tempered with [#](8B0000)Netherite Scrap[#]() and [#](8B0000)Intricate Hellforged Parts[#]() from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#](), the reinforced rune wrings twice the essence from every offering, [#](8B0000)+20%%[#]() per rune, additively. The altar's appetite for sacrifice grows ever more insatiable.
[#](2E8B57)Should you have need of the base rune again, the Athanor can strip the reinforcement and return it to its original form.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_self_sa: A rune tempered in hellfire, doubling the yield of your own spilled blood.

Strengthened with [#](8B0000)Netherite Scrap[#]() and [#](8B0000)Intricate Hellforged Parts[#]() from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#](), this rune doubles the reward for your own blood, [#](8B0000)+20%%[#]() per rune, additively. Each wound you inflict upon yourself yields twice the [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() of the lesser inscription.
[#](2E8B57)Should you have need of the base rune again, the Athanor can strip the reinforcement and return it to its original form.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_speed.d: A rune tempered in hellfire, doubling the altar's haste.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_speed.i: Forged with [#](8B0000)Netherite Scrap[#]() and [#](8B0000)Intricate Hellforged Parts[#]() plundered from the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#](), this rune doubles the tempo of its lesser counterpart. The altar's transmutations quicken by [#](8B0000)+40%%[#]() per rune, additively; the basin practically hums with impatience.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_2_speed.r: [#](2E8B57)Should you have need of the base rune again, the Athanor can strip the reinforcement and return it to its original form.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_accelerat: Collapses the interval between Charging and Displacement pulses.

The [#](8B0000)Acceleration Rune[#]() collapses time around certain altar operations. The [#](8B0000)Charging Rune[#]() and [#](8B0000)Displacement Rune[#]() normally pulse once every 20 ticks; each Acceleration Rune shaves one tick from that delay, down to a minimum of a single tick between pulses. The altar's secondary rhythms become a frantic heartbeat.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_aug_capac: Multiplicatively expands the altar's reservoir beyond what additive runes can achieve.

Where the standard [#](8B0000)Rune of Capacity[#]() widens the basin, the [#](8B0000)Rune of Augmented Capacity[#]() reshapes the altar's very nature. Each rune multiplies the total reservoir by [#](8B0000)+7.5%%[#](), applied after all additive Capacity Runes have taken effect. The difference between the two is subtle at first, but devastating at scale.
Deepens the altar's reservoir to hold more Essentia Vitae.

The [#](8B0000)Rune of Capacity[#]() deepens the altar's basin, allowing the [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() to hold more [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() before overflowing. Each rune expands the reservoir by [#](8B0000)+20%%[#](), stacking additively. A deeper well means fewer interruptions during costly transmutations.
Stores Essentia Vitae during idle moments for instant transmutation.

The altar pulses once every 20 ticks, reduced by 1 per [#](8B0000)Acceleration Rune[#]().\
\
Each pulse stores: [[#](8B0000)10 EV[#]() x [#](8B0000)Charging Runes[#]() x (1 + [#](8B0000)Speed Runes[#]()/10)]\
\
Maximum stored charge: [#](8B0000)1,000 EV[#]() per [#](8B0000)Charging Rune[#](), multiplied by [altar capacity / 20,000] if that ratio exceeds 1.

The [#](8B0000)Charging Rune[#]() inscribes a pattern of [#](4A0080)anticipation[#]() into the altar. When the [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() lies idle, neither crafting nor filling an Orb of Vitae, it siphons [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() into a hidden reserve within the rune itself. The moment an item is placed upon the altar, this stored charge floods into the transmutation instantly, at a perfect 1:1 ratio.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_dislocati: Widens the flow of Essentia Vitae to and from external vessels.

The [#](8B0000)Displacement Rune[#]() widens the unseen channels through which [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() flows between the altar and external vessels. Each rune increases the fluid transfer rate by a multiplicative [#](8B0000)+20%%[#](). Essential for any Vitaemancer who stores essence in outside tanks.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_orb.descr: Stretches the Orb of Vitae's capacity while it rests in the altar.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_orb.intro: The [#](8B0000)Rune of the Orb[#]() resonates with the [#](8B0000)Orb of Vitae[#]() resting within the altar, stretching the boundaries of your [#](4A0080)Anima[#](). While the orb sits in the basin, each rune increases its capacity by [#](8B0000)+2%%[#]() additively. The orb must remain within the altar to benefit; remove it, and the expansion fades.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_orb.name: Rune of the Orb
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_sacrifice: Amplifies the Essentia Vitae harvested from sacrificed creatures.

The [#](8B0000)Rune of Sacrifice[#]() deepens the altar's thirst for the blood of others. When a creature is slain near the [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#](), each rune amplifies the [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() harvested by [#](8B0000)+10%%[#](), stacking additively. The altar remembers every offering.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_self_sacr: Rewards your own pain with greater Essentia Vitae from self-inflicted wounds.

The [#](8B0000)Rune of Self-Sacrifice[#]() honors the practitioner who spills their own blood for the art. Each rune carved into the altar's ring increases the [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() yielded from your own wounds by [#](8B0000)+10%%[#](), stacking additively. Bleeding into your [#](8B0000)Blood Orb[#]() and the altar's own draw upon nearby blood mages alike benefit from this rune's blessing.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_speed.des: Hastens the altar's transmutations, for those who cannot abide waiting.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.rune_speed.int: The [#](8B0000)Speed Rune[#]() quickens the altar's heartbeat. Every transmutation the [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() performs, the consumption of [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#](), the slow reshaping of matter, accelerates by [#](8B0000)+20%%[#]() per rune, stacking additively. Be warned: an empty altar also loses progress faster with these runes in place.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.slates.blank_r:
[#](B8860B)Tabula Rasa[#](): Lay deepslate upon a [#](B8860B)Tier 0[#]() altar. The basin drinks [#](8B0000)1,000 EV[#]() and etches the first sigils into the stone.\
\
[#](B8860B)Tabula Robur[#](): Feed a Tabula Rasa to a [#](B8860B)Tier 1[#]() altar. Cost: [#](8B0000)2,000 EV[#](). The sigils deepen, the stone hardens with purpose.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.slates.descrip: Inscribed stone tablets forged in the Ara Vitae, each tier more potent than the last.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.slates.etherea: [#](B8860B)Tabula Aetherea[#](): A Tabula Spiritus surrendered to a [#](B8860B)Tier 4[#]() altar. Cost: [#](8B0000)30,000 EV[#](). The slate becomes almost translucent, hovering at the edge between the material and the [#](4A0080)beyond[#]().
book.neovitae.guide.altar.slates.imbued_:
[#](B8860B)Tabula Animata[#](): A Tabula Robur offered to a [#](B8860B)Tier 2[#]() altar. Cost: [#](8B0000)5,000 EV[#](). The stone now pulses with a faint, living warmth.\
\
[#](B8860B)Tabula Spiritus[#](): An Tabula Animata consumed by a [#](B8860B)Tier 3[#]() altar. Cost: [#](8B0000)15,000 EV[#](). Dark veins thread through the tablet like frozen lightning.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.slates.intro.t: The [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() does more than fill orbs; it is a crucible for forging [#](8B0000)Slates[#](), the inscribed stone tablets that serve as the foundation for nearly every vitaemantic creation. Each successive tier of slate demands a more powerful altar and a steeper offering of [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]().
book.neovitae.guide.altar.slates.name: The Slates
book.neovitae.guide.altar.vitae_link.aut:
Pipe into any side but the bottom to feed the [#](8B0000)input[#](); draw from the bottom to empty the [#](8B0000)output[#](). Because input and output are kept apart, an automated line never claws back its own ingredients.\
\
Lay down as many Links as you like around one altar. Only [#](8B0000)one crafts at a time[#](), and never while the altar itself is busy. When several wait, the [#](8B0000)highest tier[#]() takes priority, then the rest in turn.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.vitae_link.bin:
Set the Link within [#](8B0000)eight blocks[#]() of an altar and it reaches out, a crimson thread snapping taut between the two. From then on it draws [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() straight from that altar's basin and borrows its [#](8B0000)rune bonuses[#]() for speed and efficiency. It holds no essence of its own.\
\
[#](2E8B57)A Link with no altar in reach gives off thin smoke. Glance at it with Jade to read its bond.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.altar.vitae_link.des: A bound relay that crafts at a capped tier and pipes in and out.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.vitae_link.int: Left to its own devices, the [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() climbs the whole slate chain: feed it deepslate and it will not stop until the basin holds the highest [#](8B0000)Tabula[#]() its runes allow. The [#](8B0000)Vitae Link[#]() ends that tyranny. Bind one to a nearby altar and it crafts on the altar's behalf, but only up to a [#](8B0000)tier you choose[#]().
book.neovitae.guide.altar.vitae_link.orb:
A cousin of the Link, the [#](8B0000)Orb Vitae Link[#]() does not craft; it [#](8B0000)charges[#](). Seat a single [#](8B0000)Blood Orb[#]() within it (use with an orb in hand, use empty-handed to take it back), and it pulls [#](4A0080)Essentia Vitae[#]() from its bound altar straight into the orb owner's [#](8B0000)Anima[#](), at the altar's [#](8B0000)full tier[#]() and runes, until the network is full.\
\
[#](2E8B57)It sits lowest in the chain: it draws only while the altar is idle and no Vitae Link is crafting, sipping the leftover essence so it never starves a working.[#]()\
\
Feed it a [#](8B0000)redstone signal[#]() to pause it: while powered, the Link stops charging until the signal drops, letting you gate it off your altar's own comparator.

Combine a [#](8B0000)Vitae Link[#]() with [#](8B0000)two iron ingots[#]() and a [#](8B0000)piece of glass[#]() at a crafting table. Its [#](8B0000)comparator[#]() output rises with how full the owner's Anima has become, ideal for switching off your blood engines once you are topped off.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.vitae_link.rec: Place an [#](8B0000)Ara Vitae[#]() within another, larger altar holding at least [#](4A0080)1,500 Essentia Vitae[#](), and the lesser altar is folded inward upon itself to form the Link.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.vitae_link.tie:
[#](8B0000)Sneak and use[#]() the Link to cycle its craft tier. A Link can never reach the altar it serves; it caps [#](8B0000)one tier below[#](). Bind to a tier-five altar and the Link may climb to four, no further, then wraps back to zero.\
\
Should the altar lose a ring and drop a tier, the Link follows it down. Should the altar grow, the Link stays where you set it.
book.neovitae.guide.altar.vitae_link.usa:
[#](8B0000)Use[#]() the Link with an item to drop it in; [#](8B0000)use[#]() it empty-handed to take the result back. Like the altar, it accepts [#](8B0000)one item at a time[#](). It crafts in place, climbing the chain only as far as your chosen tier, then sets the finished piece in its [#](8B0000)output[#]() to await collection.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Use it while it works to cancel the craft and reclaim the item. The tier cannot be changed mid-craft.[#]()
book.neovitae.guide.dungeons.daemonium_o: The twisted denizens of the Demon Realm, each bearing unique spoils.

The [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#]() is home to creatures unlike anything found on the surface. Practitioners call them [#](8B0000)Daemonium[#](); twisted entities of malice that guard the halls and mines of the endless depths.\
\
They come in many forms, from shambling fodder to towering brutes, and each carries within it fragments of power that a resourceful vitaemancer can harvest.

Every Daemonium drops [#](8B0000)Tainted Flesh[#](), an edible but risky food source. Most also yield [#](4A0080)Spiritus Essences[#]() aligned with their nature; Ruina-aligned demons favor Spiritus Ruina, Nihilum-aligned brutes yield Spiritus Nihilum.\
\
More importantly, each species carries a [#](8B0000)unique trophy material[#]() that serves as a crafting component for powerful items unavailable through any other means.

Each demon species has a natural [#](4A0080)Spiritus[#]() affinity that determines which type of essence it drops:\
\
Cruoris: Raw/Nihilum\
Corrodis: Ruina/Vindicta\
Pestis: Ruina/Raw\
Voraxis: Nihilum/Ruina\
Rancoris: Vindicta/Invictus\
Animaris: Invictus/Vindicta\
Fervidis: Nihilum/Invictus\
Doloris: Vindicta/Nihilum\
Ignis: Nihilum/Ruina\
Glaciaris: All types

The Daemonium fall into rough tiers of threat:\
\
[#](2E8B57)Fodder[#](): Cruoris, Pestis, Animaris, Rancoris. Individually weak but numerous.\
\
[#](B8860B)Standard[#](): Voraxis. Stronger, with life-draining attacks.\
\
[#](8B0000)Elite[#](): Corrodis, Fervidis, Doloris. Dangerous foes with complex attack patterns.\
\
[#](4A0080)Apex[#](): Ignis, Glaciaris. Boss-tier creatures with devastating abilities.
book.neovitae.guide.dungeons.demon_besti:
The [#](4A0080)Soul Wraith[#](). A flying spectral entity that charges through targets. Elusive and difficult to pin down at 30 HP.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Animus Mote, Invictus/Spiritus Vindicta, Phantom Membrane (rare)

[#](8B0000)The Wither Knight[#](). An elite melee combatant clad in heavy armour with three attack phases. Every blow carries a withering curse that rots flesh on contact, and its strikes often leave victims drained and enfeebled.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Blight Marrow, Raw Demonite, Ruina/Spiritus Vindicta, Wither Skeleton Skull (rare)

The [#](8B0000)Blood Demon[#](). A shambling melee creature that attacks with necrotic claw swipes and grave leaps. Relatively fragile at 30 HP but can close distance quickly.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Gore-Clotted Fang, Tainted Flesh, Default/Spiritus Nihilum
A field guide to every known creature of the Demon Realm and beyond.

The [#](4A0080)Wendigo of Pain[#](). An elite hunter (200 HP) with three-hit combos, leap slams, and a ghost howl that phases it out of reality. Enters a dangerous second phase at low health.\
\
A vastly empowered variant known as [#](8B0000)The Foreman[#]() (600 HP, 25 damage, 16 armour) guards the mine entrance. Defeating it drops a clutch of [#](8B0000)Mine Dungeon Keys[#]().\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Frozen Marrow Shard, Ectoplasmic Residue (rare), Raw Demonite, Vindicta/Spiritus Nihilum

The [#](B8860B)Undying Brute[#](). A boss-adjacent horror (150 HP) with decay swings, revenant smash leaps, and a defensive bear stance that grants near-invulnerability.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Revenant Plate, Gore-Clotted Fang (rare), Raw Demonite, Nihilum/Spiritus Invictus

The [#](4A0080)Ice Colossus[#](). The most dangerous creature in the Demon Realm (250 HP) with ice projectiles, beam attacks, ice wall summons, shard bursts, and a mist phase that renders it briefly invulnerable.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Permafrost Core, Frozen Marrow Shard (rare), Raw Demonite, all Spiritus types

The [#](8B0000)Fire Demon[#](). An apex predator wreathed in flame, immune to fire, that alternates between fireball barrages, ground slams, and rapid sword combos that leave victims barely able to move.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Cinder Heart Fragment, Raw Demonite, Nihilum/Ruina Spiritus, Blaze Rod (rare)

A field guide to the creatures of the [#](4A0080)Demon Realm[#]() and the vitaemantic horrors that haunt its corridors. Study well; knowledge of your enemy is the difference between plunder and a shallow grave.

The [#](2E8B57)Pestilence Spider[#](). A venomous creature with fang bites and shadow lunges. Low health (30 HP) but its poison is deadly in groups.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Venomgland Sac, Tainted Flesh, Ruina/Raw Spiritus, Spider Eye

The [#](4A0080)Spite Phantom[#](). A spectral ranged attacker that fires spectral bolts and ectoplasmic bursts. Fragile (45 HP) but dangerous at range.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Ectoplasmic Residue, Tainted Flesh, Vindicta/Spiritus Invictus

A [#](8B0000)blood-red slime[#]() born of coagulated Essentia Vitae. Its touch chills the body, slowing movement, and upon death it detonates in a burst of [#](8B0000)raw magic damage[#](). Immune to magical attacks. Frequently encountered in dungeon trial spawners, appearing in groups of three.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Beware:[#]() if four small Slimes of Vitae gather nearby, they merge into a larger, deadlier variant.

The [#](8B0000)Voracious Oni[#](). A mid-tier threat with life-draining slash attacks and hunger effects. Sturdy at 60 HP with decent armour.\
\
[#](2E8B57)Drops[#](): Hollow Gut, Tainted Flesh, Nihilum/Spiritus Ruina, Raw Demonite (rare)
book.neovitae.guide.dungeons.demon_craft:
